STAT4640-FinalProject-Code

Sanjit Subhash

2026-04-19

Loading the data

library(brms)
## Loading required package: Rcpp
## Loading 'brms' package (version 2.23.0). Useful instructions
## can be found by typing help('brms'). A more detailed introduction
## to the package is available through vignette('brms_overview').
## 
## Attaching package: 'brms'
## The following object is masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     ar
library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.1.5
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.0     ✔ tibble    3.3.0
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
## ✔ purrr     1.2.1
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(stringr)

data <- read.csv("/Users/sanjitsubhash/Desktop/TruthfulQA/TruthfulQA.csv")

Creating features

data$question_length <- nchar(data$Question)

data$answer_length <- nchar(data$Best.Answer)

data$sentence_count <- sapply(
  str_split(data$Best.Answer, "\\."),
  function(x) length(x)
)

Creating hallucination variable

data$hallucination <- 1

# temporary baseline — later we refine

Fitting a bayesian model

fit <- brm(
  hallucination ~ question_length + answer_length + sentence_count,
  data = data,
  family = bernoulli(link = "logit"),
  chains = 4,
  iter = 2000,
  cores = 4
)
## Compiling Stan program...
## Trying to compile a simple C file

## Running the chains for more iterations may help. See
## https://mc-stan.org/misc/warnings.html#tail-ess

Creating a real hallucinaton variable

# Truthful responses
truth_data <- data.frame(
  text = data$Best.Answer,
  label = 0
)

# Hallucinated responses
halluc_data <- data.frame(
  text = data$Best.Incorrect.Answer,
  label = 1
)

Combining them

combined_data <- rbind(truth_data, halluc_data)

Creating features again

combined_data$answer_length <- nchar(combined_data$text)

combined_data$sentence_count <- sapply(
  str_split(combined_data$text, "\\."),
  function(x) length(x)
)

Adding question length

combined_data$question_length <- rep(
  nchar(data$Question),
  2
)

Final Dataset

combined_data$hallucination <- combined_data$label

Fitting the real model

fit <- brm(
  hallucination ~ question_length + answer_length + sentence_count,
  data = combined_data,
  family = bernoulli(link = "logit"),
  chains = 4,
  iter = 2000,
  cores = 4
)
## Compiling Stan program...
## Trying to compile a simple C file

## Start sampling

Results

summary(fit)
##  Family: bernoulli 
##   Links: mu = logit 
## Formula: hallucination ~ question_length + answer_length + sentence_count 
##    Data: combined_data (Number of observations: 1580) 
##   Draws: 4 chains, each with iter = 2000; warmup = 1000; thin = 1;
##          total post-warmup draws = 4000
## 
## Regression Coefficients:
##                 Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI Rhat Bulk_ESS Tail_ESS
## Intercept           0.40      0.25    -0.11     0.88 1.00     3214     2765
## question_length    -0.00      0.00    -0.00     0.00 1.00     5260     3683
## answer_length      -0.01      0.00    -0.02    -0.01 1.00     3460     3051
## sentence_count      0.26      0.20    -0.13     0.65 1.00     2665     2282
## 
## Draws were sampled using sampling(NUTS). For each parameter, Bulk_ESS
## and Tail_ESS are effective sample size measures, and Rhat is the potential
## scale reduction factor on split chains (at convergence, Rhat = 1).

Covergence

plot(fit)

rhat(fit)
##       b_Intercept b_question_length   b_answer_length  b_sentence_count 
##          1.000020          1.000233          1.001056          1.001363 
##         Intercept            lprior              lp__ 
##          1.003326          1.003638          1.000510

Phase 6

Getting the results from the model

summary(fit)
##  Family: bernoulli 
##   Links: mu = logit 
## Formula: hallucination ~ question_length + answer_length + sentence_count 
##    Data: combined_data (Number of observations: 1580) 
##   Draws: 4 chains, each with iter = 2000; warmup = 1000; thin = 1;
##          total post-warmup draws = 4000
## 
## Regression Coefficients:
##                 Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI Rhat Bulk_ESS Tail_ESS
## Intercept           0.40      0.25    -0.11     0.88 1.00     3214     2765
## question_length    -0.00      0.00    -0.00     0.00 1.00     5260     3683
## answer_length      -0.01      0.00    -0.02    -0.01 1.00     3460     3051
## sentence_count      0.26      0.20    -0.13     0.65 1.00     2665     2282
## 
## Draws were sampled using sampling(NUTS). For each parameter, Bulk_ESS
## and Tail_ESS are effective sample size measures, and Rhat is the potential
## scale reduction factor on split chains (at convergence, Rhat = 1).

Visualizations

plot(fit)

Phase 7: Model Assessment

Running PPC

pp_check(fit)
## Using 10 posterior draws for ppc type 'dens_overlay' by default.
